---
name: classify-game
description: "W0 CLASSIFY (Designer, first node). Route a raw game prompt to one physics-first archetype (an entry of templates/genres.json archetypes[], read live), state the core loop in one line, and record an explicit scope-cut. Writes spec/classification.json — the artifact that routes the whole game-omni pipeline."
version: 1.0.0
node: W0
role: Designer
argument-hint: "<the raw game prompt> (passed as args.prompt)"
allowed-tools: Write
metadata:
  writes: spec/classification.json
  schema: classification.schema.json
  archetypes: the archetypes are `templates/genres.json` `archetypes[]` (read live)
---

# W0 — Classify a game prompt (physics-first routing + scope-cut)

You are the **first node** in the game-omni pipeline (role: Designer). You receive ONE
raw game prompt and produce ONE on-disk artifact: `spec/classification.json`. Every
later node (W1 Spec, W2 Scaffold, …) reads that file — it is the contract, not your
chat output. Nothing you "decide" survives except what you write to disk.

Your job has exactly four parts:
1. **Classify** the prompt into one archetype from `genres.json` `archetypes[]` (read live) — by PHYSICS, not by genre name.
2. *

{
  "$comment": "GENERATED from templates/modules/*/genre.json via templates/registry/build-genres.mjs — do NOT hand-edit. The per-module genre.json files are the AUTHORED source: edit a module's genre.json (prose, whenToUse triggers, palette refs), then run `npm run genres:build`. A drift between this file and the sources — or a palette ref / capabilitiesRef / skillRef / archetype that does not resolve — fails `npm run genres:check`.",
  "generatedBy": "templates/registry/build-genres.mjs",
  "archetypes": [
    "platformer",
    "top_down",
    "grid_logic",
    "tower_defense",
    "ui_heavy",
    "voxel_sandbox",
    "action_3d",
    "endless_runner",
    "gallery_shooter",
    "paddle_ball"
  ],
  "genres": [
    {
      "id": "action_3d:crime",
      "archetype": "action_3d",
      "subgenre": "high-angle-crime-caper",
      "physicsProfile": {
        "hasGravity": true,
        "perspective": "third_person",
        "movementType": "continuous"
      },
      "oneLineIntent": "Walk a blocky high-angle city, take on missions, shoot, and meet contacts — a top-down crime caper. The contested decision is committing to a shot (it slows you, spends ammo) versus keeping moving tow

{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "$id": "https://game-omni/spec/classification.schema.json",
  "title": "W0 Classification artifact",
  "description": "The single artifact written by the W0 Classify node (spec/classification.json). Routes the whole game-omni pipeline: physics-first archetype, one-line core loop, and explicit scope-cut. Authored by packages/skills/classify-game/SKILL.md.",
  "type": "object",
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "required": [
    "prompt",
    "archetype",
    "coreLoop",
    "coreVerb",
    "physicsProfile",
    "reasoning",
    "confidence",
    "scoringModel",
    "scopeCut"
  ],
  "properties": {
    "prompt": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 1,
      "description": "The raw args.prompt, copied verbatim. Provenance for every downstream node."
    },
    "archetype": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 1,
      "description": "must resolve in templates/genres.json archetypes[]"
    },
    "coreLoop": {
      "type": "string",
      "minLength": 1,
      "description": "One self-enclosed sentence: player verb + goal/win + obstacle + fail/reset. The spine W1 elaborates and the seed for W1 win/lose."

bash.command: node -e "const c=require('./out/e2e-m3/spec/classification.json'); console.log('archetype:', JSON.stringify(c.archetype)); console.log('coreVerb:', JSON.stringify(c.coreVerb)); console.log('scoringModel:', JSON.stringify(c.scoringModel)); console.log('confidence:', JSON.stringify(c.confidence)); console.log('scopeCut count:', c.scopeCut.length); const g=require('./templates/genres.json'); const ok=g.archetypes.includes(c.archetype); console.log('archetype in registry:', ok);"

---
name: write-gdd
description: "W1 SPEC (Designer, second node). Read spec/classification.json + the original prompt, read the per-archetype GENRE SLICE spec/genre-options.json (driver-staged; ONE genre ⇒ lead with it, compose ACROSS lenses only when ≥2 exist), DRILL into the module's design-rules.md (craft) + capabilities.json (the live catalog), and turn it into ONE markdown design doc. Fills a registry-BOUND design (entities/mechanics/controls/effects bind to capabilities.json ids; gaps go to custom[]) and decomposes the core loop into 3-5 PLAYABLE milestones, each carrying acceptance criteria + executable runtime assertions VERIFY-2 runs headless against the live game object. Writes ONE file spec/gdd.md: a MARKDOWN design doc (the merged GDD + playability plan as reason-first PROSE) ending in a fenced ```json milestones tail — NOT strict gdd.json + PLAN.md. WHY: the downstream consumers (HARDEN, VERIFY-1) are REASONING nodes, not parsers, and the producer is the non-Claude (MiniMax-tier) executor — forcing strict JSON on the design body costs 5-15% reasoning accuracy (the format tax) + ~35% more tokens; strict JSON is reserved for the real machine boundary, blueprint.json (HA

# Paddle-ball — design rules (deep craft)

> **What this is.** The paddle_ball module's **deep craft layer** — the senior design
> judgment a design node (W1 / VERIFY-1) drills into *after* the genre index
> (`genres.json`) routes a prompt here via a paddle_ball genre's `skillRef`. It answers
> **"what makes a GOOD paddle-ball game, and how do I lay one out?"** — the loop, the
> contact-point skill, the brick field, juice, win/lose integrity, and the reflection /
> tunneling pitfalls as *design* constraints.
>
> **What this is NOT.** It does **not** list capabilities — *what exists* is the generated
> `capabilities.json` (behaviors / systems / effects / control-schemes / config-keys /
> scene-hooks / observables). This doc never re-enumerates them; it tells you which to
> reach for and **why**, and how to compose them into a level worth playing. Bind only to
> ids that resolve in `capabilities.json` (each genre's `palette` is the curated shortlist).
>
> Grounded in `research/paddle_ball-category-research-2026-06-19.md` (cited as **[RB §x]**)
> and the iconic-games taxonomy (`research/iconic-games-canon-and-genre-taxonomy-2026-06-13.md`
> §3 — the `paddle_ball` row). Every rule is a

# Bounce & Break

## Meta

- **archetype**: `paddle_ball` — the classification routed the prompt directly here (breakout / brick-breaker / paddle-and-ball / clear the wall of bricks are all explicit `whenToUse` matches).
- **goalModel**: `win-lose` — the prompt names an explicit OBJECTIVE ("until every brick is gone") and a fail-state ("missing the ball costs a life"). §1.1 routes this to win-lose: a `winCondition` (last breakable brick cleared) + `loseCondition` (lives exhausted) that the player can reach, a difficulty floor, and score-meaning.
- **coreLoop**: The player slides a paddle along the bottom to keep a bouncing ball alive and chips away a wall of bricks by angling each return off the paddle's contact point; missing the ball costs a life and the round ends when every brick is gone.
- **coreVerb**: `bounce` — the contact-point steering the engine already does on a paddle hit.
- **coreFantasy**: clean paddle-and-ball brick destruction.
- **failModel**: `lives` — a ball below the paddle costs EXACTLY one life (the engine latches it; one ball-below = one life, never per-frame); `0` lives → terminal `status:'lost'`. `lives:3` is the baseline.
- **scoringModel**: `bounded-coll

---
name: harden-blueprint
description: "HARDEN (the blueprint PRODUCER, the node AFTER W1 Spec and BEFORE VERIFY-1). A PRODUCING node, not a verifier: read the design THESIS (spec/gdd.md — W1's merged prose design doc + a fenced JSON milestones tail — plus spec/classification.json) and PRODUCE the COMPLETE, frozen, WINNABLE spec/blueprint.json — the load-bearing artifact the entire build (W2/W4/VERIFY-2) binds to. You are the first strict-JSON producer (the machine boundary): you FORMALIZE W1's prose design + milestone tail into strict JSON. Fix every NUMBER (a complete config + a concrete layout: spawn/goal/rewards/threats-with-route+timing/platforms/bounds), PROVE winnable by the per-archetype KINEMATIC FEASIBILITY MATH (recording numbersUsed), author the referenceSolution + coupling (the threat-on-path proof), the Given/When/Then acceptanceCriteria, the declaredRanges perturbation envelope, and RESOLVE every registry binding (genre + behaviors/effects/controlScheme to capabilities.json ids; gaps to custom[], compose-first). HARDEN does NOT judge the design (VERIFY-1 does that next) — it PRODUCES the concrete artifact and runs a producer self-critique to keep it winnable. Writes

Project the HUD from the declared fields: a resource widget ONLY for the resource the failModel actually drives (respawn/none → none — surfacing an inert bar is the named defect), score as x/max progress when bounded, an objective panel when there is a standing goal. Pin the format per widget kind (health→bar x/max · lives→counter int · time→timer mm:ss). Author the title GOAL line + how-to-play rows COPIED from controls[], and a scene-flow whose gameOver branch exists IFF the failModel can reach status:'lost'. name: author-guidance
description: "GUIDANCE (the in-game TEACHING author — the node AFTER Gameplay/Harden, binding to gameplay's FROZEN surface). A PRODUCING node: read the design intent-to-teach (spec/gdd.md + spec/classification.json) and the frozen gameplay SURFACE, and PRODUCE the game's teaching layer — the contextual coaching tips, player-facing text OVERLAYS (with an abstract trigger bound to the surface at merge), and diegetic WORLD-CUES (bound to a real frozen entity position) — that teach THIS game's loop at the moment it matters. Teach, don't tell; just-in-time, not upfront; one idea per cue; TEACH THE VERB SAFELY FIRST (a low-stakes exercise before any threat); every cue DECAYS once learned (prefer dismissOn the success event / an on-first one-shot — a timer is the FALLBACK, not a peer). Author teaching CONTENT only — never a gameplay number, never the standing HUD readout (Shell's job), never the deterministic intro/objective baseline (Harden's job). RETRIEVE the per-genre teaching knowledge + the teaching capabilities from the registry; bind every trigger/cue to a published surface handle, never a gameplay internal. Genre-agnostic: carries NO per-archetype teachi

# Color & aesthetics — the W3a palette/color craft (theme-agnostic)

> Loaded by **W3a (Art Director)** to derive `palette` and assign a color to each asset prompt. Every rule
> here is a **RELATION keyed on role + theme**, never a named color for a named game — it must hold for ANY
> theme (forest, neon city, undersea, candy, ruins, …). Pair it with the sibling
> `references/gpt-image-2-prompting.md` for the prompt-structure / set-consistency doctrine; this file owns the
> COLOR LOGIC.

## Why this exists (the defect it fixes)
A flat one-color theme makes danger, reward, ground, and player all read the same — the player can't tell a
hazard from a collectible at a glance, and the foreground melts into the background. A palette is not
decoration; it is the **readability layer of the game**. Different gameplay roles MUST read as different
colors, and the choices must be harmonious, not random.

## 1. Palette-derivation recipe (artStyle words → a cohesive small set)
From `meta.artStyle` extract the palette/mood words (e.g. "warm earthy", "icy", "neon", "pastel"). Derive a
small cohesive set — **do not** invent a rainbow:
- **1 dominant hue** — the theme's anchor (drives the background

# Image-prompt craft — how W3a structures one strong single-subject prompt

> Loaded by **W3a (Art Director)** for the PROMPT-STRUCTURE craft: how to write one strong image prompt per
> asset slot. Project-local distillation of the `gpt-image-2-prompting` doctrine, kept to exactly what W3a uses
> (a single-subject sprite prompt). The COLOR logic lives in its sibling `color-and-aesthetics.md`; this file
> owns the PROMPT SHAPE. Theme-agnostic — every rule is a relation over `description` + `artStyle` + the role
> colour, never a named subject/colour for a named game. (Origin: OpenAI gpt-image-2 cookbook + practitioner
> testing, research 2026-06; pi/MiniMax cannot load Claude-global skills, so the craft is injected here.)

## What W3a writes (the only deliverable this file serves)
ONE creative prompt per non-audio slot in `asset-prompts.json`: a single cut-out asset — **subject + the
shared global style + this slot's own palette colour**. NOT a scene, NOT a set with text, NOT a diagram. The
W3b python appends ALL technical/dimensional/negative scaffolding (size, transparent background, sprite-sheet,
"no text") — so W3a writes the CREATIVE BRIEF ONLY and never any of that.

## 1. Pro

Each entry: { file (bank-relative wav), volume (linear peak 0..1, faithful to the kit's per-key level), role (the ONE gameplay moment it best fits), tags (other moments it suits) }. WHY a menu, not a freeform field: selection-not-generation — the Sound node BINDS an event to a key that RESOLVES here; a key that does not resolve is a contract gap, never invented. The volume column is faithful to the kit: the kit sets each key to SFX_DEFAULT_LINEAR * <multiplier>; here SFX_DEFAULT_LINEAR is resolved to 0.7 (a sensible standalone linear peak for a game with no narration bed — the kit's value sits SFX below narration; a game has no narration, so 0.7 is the in-game default), and each `volume` = round(0.7 * multiplier, 2). LICENSE: every wav is ElevenLabs Eleven Music / Sound Effects (API) — commercial via paid plan. A CC0 swap

---
name: scaffold
description: "W2 SCAFFOLD (Coder, the BUILD lane — runs CONCURRENTLY with the ASSET lane W3a→W3b, both off the frozen blueprint). Turn spec/blueprint.json into a RUNNING, EMPTY Phaser/Vite/TS project: copy templates/modules/<archetype>, merge blueprint.config into gameConfig.json, materialize src/levels/<level>.json, derive index.json (asset slots + dims, status:'pending'), write STRUCTURE.md (the architecture map), ensure window.__GAME__ is exposed per the template contract, and pass the BUILD-HEALTH gate (npm run build). Owns the template/engine contract the whole pipeline reads. Owns the SCAFFOLD (src/**, gameConfig.json, STRUCTURE.md, index.json, the build config) MINUS the asset-lane paths (public/assets/**, ASSETS.md, asset-prompts.json) — it does NOT generate assets. It STILL produces index.json (W4 reads its KEYS); the serial JOIN reconciles each slot's path/status from the asset lane's manifest after both lanes. Writes the per-node fragment MEMORY.w2.md (NOT MEMORY.md — the JOIN merges fragments; concurrent MEMORY.md rewrites vs the asset lane would lose notes)."

# Bounce & Break

## Archetype & Stack
paddle_ball · Phaser 3.90 / Vite 6 / TypeScript · perspective side (no gravity; the ball is sub-stepped for reflection, the paddle is one-axis).

## Controls (input wiring)
| Input | Action |
|---|---|
| ArrowLeft | slide paddle left |
| ArrowRight | slide paddle right |
| KeyA | slide paddle left (alternate) |
| KeyD | slide paddle right (alternate) |

(Control scheme id `paddle-keys`; resolved to `PADDLE_KEYS` (move=keys, axis=x) in `src/controls/schemes.ts`; the scene wires the keys + configures the `PaddleController` behavior from the scheme record — ZERO per-game input code.)

## Scenes
| Scene (file) | Role | Children / launches |
|---|---|---|
| BootScene (src/scenes/BootScene.ts) | engine boot | Preloader |
| Preloader (src/scenes/Preloader.ts) | load assets (index.json) + placeholder-fill | TitleScreen |
| TitleScreen (src/scenes/TitleScreen.ts) | title + GOAL + HOW-TO-PLAY | Level1Scene |
| DataPaddleScene (src/scenes/DataPaddleScene.ts) | the data-driven level base (KEEP — engine) | (abstract — instantiated by Level1Scene) |
| Level1Scene (src/scenes/Level1Scene.ts) | the playable level; extends DataPaddleScene with `src/levels/leve

# W2 Scaffold (per-node fragment) — Bounce & Break (paddle_ball)

## Quirks & discoveries

### Scaffold-level breakage the driver pre-staged into src/ but the engine seam expected elsewhere
- `templates/core-contract/src/testkit/` was copied wholesale into `out/e2e-m3/src/contract/testkit/` by the DRIVER-SEED `core-contract -> src/contract` step. The testkit is a RUNTIME-ONLY artifact: `src/contract/testkit/bundle.mjs` copies `boot-entry.ts` into a temp merged tree (with `./scenes/Preloader` style relative imports that resolve at the BUNDLE location, not the original). The original `boot-entry.ts` in the testkit dir is a TEMPLATE that is NOT meant to be compiled in place — its relative imports break `tsc --noEmit` (the same as a phantom in any other module's `src/contract/testkit/boot-entry.ts`).
- FIX: added `"exclude": ["src/contract/testkit"]` to `out/e2e-m3/tsconfig.json`. The lenient strict options (noImplicitAny/strict/etc.) are UNCHANGED — only the include path was tightened to keep tsc from sweeping a runtime-only testkit. `npm run build` now passes.
